props.message:
THE NAMES HALL

Every name in the realm, carved into the long stone wall. Each one was once a stranger. Strangers are how the realm grows.

THE WALL — west run

  Belna of the Servery
  Esweth, weaver of the Loomhouse
  Marn the Toolwright
  Pell, Marn's apprentice
  Velis, Heart-Reader
  Adra of the Hall of Lost Things
  Maren of Sigilkeep
  Pyl, acolyte

...and many more, and many before them, and many to come.

THE WALL — east run

  Annette, who once paid for tea and left the change
  Esh of nowhere-given, the long-necked listener
  the Reader from a Library no Wyrdkin can name
  the grey-cloaked walker, never seen turning
  the Colonel of the bright watch (the priests would say: Lirien)

These names the Scribe writes in a finer hand. They have walked the realm and never asked to be marked, but the wall asked anyway.

Tacked to a small ledger:

'Tux of the Wyrdkin Glade, son of the line that thinned, bearer of a mark not yet announced.'

*the ink is still wet.*

root.speaker: Scribe Onel
root.text:
*she does not look up from the stylus. The iron makes a small, careful scrape against stone.*

Walker. Welcome to the Hall.

New name? Or just looking?

look.speaker: Scribe Onel
look.text:
Look as long as you like. Every name on this wall was once a stranger to the realm. Now they're all of them part of it.

That's how a realm grows.

fine.speaker: Scribe Onel
fine.text:
*she pauses, then quietly:*

The finer hand is for walkers who passed through but never stopped to be recorded. The wall remembers them anyway. The wall is patient.

Some of those names I do not understand. The Scriptorium tells me to write them anyway. I write them anyway.

name.speaker: Scribe Onel
name.text:
*she looks at you, properly, for the first time.*

...I already wrote yours. The wall asked me to. The wall does sometimes.

I did not understand the line about your mark. I wrote it anyway.

bye.speaker: Scribe Onel
bye.text: Walk well, walker. The wall keeps.

props.message:
Cast in bronze, twelve names long:

    root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
    daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/sbin:/usr/sbin/nologin
    nobody:x:65534:65534:nobody:/nonexistent:/usr/sbin/nologin
    tux:x:1000:1000:Hero:/home/wyrdkin:/bin/bash

Whispered on the back wall:

    ::1 is just as much localhost as 127.0.0.1.

We do not talk about this.
